objections.location: Section 2 (tokens.css reference) + Section 0 (design token system deliverable)
objections.description: Design token system is referenced throughout but actual token values are never defined. tokens.css is listed as a deliverable but the spec contains zero color values, spacing scale, or typography scale. Cannot implement premium/confident aesthetic without concrete token definitions. References like --accent-default, --surface-0, --text-tertiary, --font-size-lg, --space-4 appear without any actual CSS custom property values.
objections.suggested_fix: Add Section 6: Design Token System with complete CSS custom property definitions including: color palette (dark/light theme pairs with hex values), spacing scale (0-12 or similar), typography scale (xs through 2xl with line-height), motion tokens (duration-fast/normal/slow, easing curves). Example: --accent-default: #8B5CF6; --surface-0: #0F0F11; --text-primary: #FAFAFA; --space-4: 1rem; --font-size-lg: 1.125rem; --duration-fast: 150ms;

objections.location: Section 3 (Tab bar visual states) + Section 4 (Component architecture)
objections.description: Color contrast ratios not specified for any interactive elements. WCAG 2.1 AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text, 3:1 for UI components. Spec mentions --text-tertiary for inactive tabs but provides no contrast guarantee. Premium aesthetic cannot ship with accessibility violations.
objections.suggested_fix: Add contrast ratio requirements to token definitions. Document minimum contrast ratios for each text variant against its background. Example: --text-tertiary on --surface-1 must achieve 4.5:1 minimum. Include automated contrast check in CI using tokens.css values.

objections.description: Motion system is listed as a deliverable but no motion tokens are defined. Spec bans ease/linear timing but doesn't specify what easing curves TO use. No duration scale, no easing function definitions, no animation naming convention.
objections.suggested_fix: Add motion token section: --duration-fast: 150ms, --duration-normal: 250ms, --duration-slow: 400ms; --ease-out-expo: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); --ease-in-out-quad: cubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1). Document which durations apply to which interaction types (hover, tap, navigation, sheet).

objections.description: Font weights defined (regular, medium, semibold) but no font size scale beyond xs (12px) and lg mentions. Premium aesthetic requires complete type scale with consistent ratios. No line-height specifications which directly impacts readability and visual hierarchy.
objections.suggested_fix: Define complete typography scale: --font-size-xs: 0.75rem (12px, line-height 1.4), --font-size-sm: 0.875rem (14px, lh 1.4), --font-size-base: 1rem (16px, lh 1.5), --font-size-lg: 1.125rem (18px, lh 1.5), --font-size-xl: 1.25rem (20px, lh 1.4), --font-size-2xl: 1.5rem (24px, lh 1.3). Specify which weights apply to which sizes.

objections.description: Icon stroke widths described as 'size-specific' but no actual values provided. Phosphor-style icons typically use 1.5px or 2px strokes. Inconsistent stroke widths across icon set will break visual hierarchy.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize icon stroke width: 1.5px for 16px icons, 2px for 24px icons. Document in icons/README.md. Ensure all SVG icons use stroke='currentColor' and consistent viewBox dimensions.

suggestions.description: Add visual regression testing to migration checklist. Before Step M5 (_legacy removal), run screenshot comparison between Alpine and React implementations for all three key screens to catch visual drift.

Add visual hierarchy documentation to each screen component. Specify which element gets visual priority (largest, highest contrast, top position) for Home, Search, and Detail screens to ensure consistent premium aesthetic.
Specify sheet elevation using shadow tokens rather than opacity alone. Premium aesthetic requires subtle depth: --shadow-sheet: 0 -4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.24) for dark mode, adjusted for light mode.
